questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. The sea has become / a cheap dumping ground / for all kind / of waste products.
questions.explanation: “for all kinds of” in place of “for all kind of” since all is plural, kind must be in its plural form.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. The Interlocking system coordinates the functions of these three components to control train movements B. It ensures that train movements continue without any conflicts with each other, preventing accidents. C Interlocking in railway signalling systems is a crucial safety mechanism used in the operation of train movements on railway tracks. D. There are three main components that comprise an interlocking system: the point, the track occupancy sensing devices, and the signal.
questions.explanation: Sentence A – “ these three components” so the preceding sentence must have the three components. From here we can say A will follow D.First sentence is either introductory or a general statement. Here, C is the introductory statement for the interlocking railway signalling system. So, CBDA is the correct sequence.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The woman bought a parrot that could speak five languages.
options: A parrot that could speak five languages was bought by the woman.
A parrot that could spoke five languages bought the woman.
A parrot that spoken five languages was bought by the woman.
A parrot that spoke five languages had been bought by the woman.
questions.explanation: To change to passive voice: • Identify the object: a parrot that could speak five languages • Use the appropriate form of the verb “to be” + past participle: was bought • Keep the subject (the woman) at the end with “by” So, the active sentence: The woman bought a parrot that could speak five languages. Becomes: A parrot that could speak five languages was bought by the woman.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Advertising companies often use rhyme and ______.
questions.explanation: alteration – change in characteristics of somethingDesignation - a name that is given to somebody for the job they do or the position they haveDenunciation – public criticism of somebody or something Alliteration – a figure of speech in which a letter or word is repeatedly used.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select ‘No substitution required’. H̲e̲a̲r̲s̲ ̲t̲o̲ the news tonight at 8 p.m., as there will be an important announcement about the budget.
questions.explanation: Hearing is a natural process in which we consciously do not involve or concentrate our mind whereas listening is a conscious process in which we bring our attention to what is being said. So, here listen to would be correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Black Sheep
options: a person who is considered as a disgrace to his family
questions.explanation: Black Sheep – a person who is considered as a disgrace to his family.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. “How beautiful she is!” said Bobby.
options: Bobby exclaimed with joy how beautiful she is.
Bobby exclaimed with joy that she was very beautiful.
Bobby exclaimed with joy how beautiful she was.
Bobby exclaimed with joy she is very beautiful.
questions.explanation: This is an exclamatory sentence expressing joy. So the reported speech will use “exclaimed with joy”. The inverted commas should be replaced with that . is should be changed to was because of the past tense. Rest should be changed according to assertive sentences.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the highlight segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select ‘No substitution’. Either the bears or the lion have escaped from the zoo.
questions.explanation: when two subjects are joined by either…….or , neither……nor, not only …….but also, ….nor ….., or….. none-but, the verb agrees with the nearest subject.So, in the given sentence, the verb should agree with “the lion”. Hence, option b is correct.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Won’t you / please come / to help me / along this heavy briefcase?
questions.explanation: along with this heavy briefcase should be used in place of along this heavy briefcase. “along with” means in company with.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the right order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Apart from the inactivity, stress is another contributor to obesity. B. Years of such exposure can have a tangible impact on a policeman’s mental and physical well being. C. In India, police personnel have long, often uncertain hours of duty, with little relief and low pay. D. Furthermore, on a daily basis, cops are exposed to stressful situations, from violent crime to managing massive crowds.
questions.explanation: A is the first statement as it brings the topic of stress and obesity into discussion…C and D both talk about cops’ exposure but D has furthermore which means it should follow C. B talks about the impact of the exposure; hence, it should be at last. So, the correct sequence is ACDB.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Social ………………., overcrowding, the competitiveness of our society and several other factors are responsible for stress.
questions.explanation: sentence is talking about stress. Social isolation can only fit into the sentence out of the given words.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. One who studies or make observations of celestial phenomena.
questions.explanation: Numerologist – study of numbers believing they have a special importance in people’s lives.Prophet – god’s messengerAstrologer – a person who studies positions of sun, moon, stars etc and their influence on humans.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Masticate
questions.explanation: Burp – a belchDrew - to move in the direction mentioned

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Commiseration
questions.explanation: Commiseration – sympathy for the misfortune of othersConviction - action of finding somebody guiltyConsolation – sympathy for misfortune of others Conduction – the process by which heat or electricity passes through a material

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: aquarium.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Something very large in size, degree, or amount,
questions.explanation: Vigorous – energeticTempestuous – full of strong emotions Sumptuous – showing wealth

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Audacious
questions.explanation: Audacious – willing to take risksStout - a fat personRobust – strong and healthyCowardly – in a coward manner

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Big gun
questions.explanation: Big gun – an influential person

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Slur
questions.explanation: slur – an unfair comment or an insult Accolade – a comment of praise, prize, etc for accomplishmentAssortment – a mixture

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Unpretentious incriminate – to provide evidence to prove someone guiltyunpretentious – not trying to show offembodiment – someone or something that represents an idea or a quality

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. Water, a substance composed of the chemical elements hydrogen and oxygen (1)______ in gaseous, liquid, and solid states. It is (2)______ of the most plentiful and essential (3)______ compounds. A tasteless and odourless liquid at room temperature, it has the important (4)______ to dissolve many other substances. Indeed, the (5)______ of water as a solvent is essential to living organisms. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: exists is the most appropriate word for the given sentence.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. Water, a substance composed of the chemical elements hydrogen and oxygen (1)______ in gaseous, liquid, and solid states. It is (2)______ of the most plentiful and essential (3)______ compounds. A tasteless and odourless liquid at room temperature, it has the important (4)______ to dissolve many other substances. Indeed, the (5)______ of water as a solvent is essential to living organisms. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: one of the most plentiful. Any other option cannot be used with “…..of the most”

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. Water, a substance composed of the chemical elements hydrogen and oxygen (1)______ in gaseous, liquid, and solid states. It is (2)______ of the most plentiful and essential (3)______ compounds. A tasteless and odourless liquid at room temperature, it has the important (4)______ to dissolve many other substances. Indeed, the (5)______ of water as a solvent is essential to living organisms. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: “of” is used to show something belongs to a group.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. Water, a substance composed of the chemical elements hydrogen and oxygen (1)______ in gaseous, liquid, and solid states. It is (2)______ of the most plentiful and essential (3)______ compounds. A tasteless and odourless liquid at room temperature, it has the important (4)______ to dissolve many other substances. Indeed, the (5)______ of water as a solvent is essential to living organisms. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: ability is the most appropriate word out of the given form in the given sentence.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. Water, a substance composed of the chemical elements hydrogen and oxygen (1)______ in gaseous, liquid, and solid states. It is (2)______ of the most plentiful and essential (3)______ compounds. A tasteless and odourless liquid at room temperature, it has the important (4)______ to dissolve many other substances. Indeed, the (5)______ of water as a solvent is essential to living organisms. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: versatility – ability to adapt to different functions Veracity - the quality of being true or correct.Versatility is the most appropriate word to fill in the blank.
